input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

Replace every hardcoded color with an `AppThemeBinding` that supplies a value for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#B39DDB}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the OS theme at startup **and** listens for changes — no event wiring needed. When the user toggles system dark mode, every `AppThemeBinding` re-evaluates automatically.

### C# alternative — `SetAppTheme<T>()`

The extension method `SetAppTheme` does the same thing from code-behind:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
BackgroundColor = Colors.White;
this.SetAppTheme(BackgroundColorProperty, Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

var heading = new Label { Text = "Welcome!", FontSize = 28 };
heading.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));
```

**Signature:** `SetAppTheme<T>(BindableProperty, T lightValue, T darkValue)`

### Tips

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Resources** | You can theme via `AppThemeBinding` inside a `ResourceDictionary` / `Style` so every control picks it up globally. |
| **Detection** | Read the current theme anytime with `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (`AppTheme.Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`). |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of the OS setting. |
| **Change event** | Subscribe to `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` if you need to run logic beyond re-binding. |

That's all you need — swap each hardcoded color for an `AppThemeBinding` (XAML) or `SetAppTheme` call (C#), and the page becomes fully theme-aware with no additional plumbing.
